title: cyathea strigillosa
intro: Cyathea strigillosa, also known as the Strigose Tree Fern, is a species of tree fern native to Australia and New Guinea. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Cyathea strigillosa include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Cyathea strigillosa can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
